hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'Sanguis' is addressed directly — it is vocative
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'sanguis' would be 'sanguinis' — 'Sanguis' here is vocative
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'sanguis' would be 'sanguinem' — 'Sanguis' is vocative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'sanguinis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'sanguinis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'sanguinis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'Christus' — 'Christi' has the -i genitive ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'Christus' would be 'Christo' — 'Christi' ends in -i (genitive)
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'Christus' would be 'Christe' — 'Christi' is genitive

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 3rd sg. would be 'inebriat' — 'inebria' is the imperative singular
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'inebries' — 'inebria' is the imperative singular
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'inebriare' — 'inebria' is the imperative singular

hints.bibo, bibere, bibi, bibitum: These are the principal parts of 'bibere' (to drink)
hints.intro, intrare, intravi, intratum: These are the principal parts of 'intrare' (to enter)
hints.poto, potare, potavi, potatum: These are the principal parts of 'potare' (to drink)
